verses.text: Beware 'the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy'; nothing hidden shall not be revealed. No hiding. All secret things shall be made known.
Beware 'the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy'; nothing hidden shall not be revealed. No hiding. All secret things shall be made known.
Beware 'the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy'; nothing hidden shall not be revealed. No hiding. All secret things shall be made known.
Fear not them that kill the body, but Him who can cast into hell; not a sparrow is forgotten. The right fear. Awe of God drives out the fear of man.
Fear not them that kill the body, but Him who can cast into hell; not a sparrow is forgotten. The right fear. Awe of God drives out the fear of man.
Fear not them that kill the body, but Him who can cast into hell; not a sparrow is forgotten. The right fear. Awe of God drives out the fear of man.
Fear not them that kill the body, but Him who can cast into hell; not a sparrow is forgotten. The right fear. Awe of God drives out the fear of man.
Confess Me before men, and the Son of man shall confess you; blasphemy against the Spirit is not forgiven. Open allegiance. To own Christ now is to be owned by Him then.
Confess Me before men, and the Son of man shall confess you; blasphemy against the Spirit is not forgiven. Open allegiance. To own Christ now is to be owned by Him then.
Confess Me before men, and the Son of man shall confess you; blasphemy against the Spirit is not forgiven. Open allegiance. To own Christ now is to be owned by Him then.
Take no thought what to answer; 'the Holy Ghost shall teach you... what ye ought to say.' Promised help. God gives words to His witnesses in need.
Take no thought what to answer; 'the Holy Ghost shall teach you... what ye ought to say.' Promised help. God gives words to His witnesses in need.
Asked to divide an inheritance, He warns, 'beware of covetousness'; life is not in abundance. Guard the heart. A man's true life lies not in his possessions.
Asked to divide an inheritance, He warns, 'beware of covetousness'; life is not in abundance. Guard the heart. A man's true life lies not in his possessions.
Asked to divide an inheritance, He warns, 'beware of covetousness'; life is not in abundance. Guard the heart. A man's true life lies not in his possessions.
A rich man's ground brought forth plenty, and he planned bigger barns. Self-centred. He thinks only of himself and this life.
A rich man's ground brought forth plenty, and he planned bigger barns. Self-centred. He thinks only of himself and this life.
A rich man's ground brought forth plenty, and he planned bigger barns. Self-centred. He thinks only of himself and this life.
'Soul... take thine ease'; 'Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required.' So is he not rich toward God. Sudden reckoning. To hoard for self and forget God is folly.
'Soul... take thine ease'; 'Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required.' So is he not rich toward God. Sudden reckoning. To hoard for self and forget God is folly.
'Soul... take thine ease'; 'Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required.' So is he not rich toward God. Sudden reckoning. To hoard for self and forget God is folly.
Take no thought for life; consider the ravens, which God feeds; ye are better than the fowls. Trustful ease. God's care for the birds assures us of His care for us.
Take no thought for life; consider the ravens, which God feeds; ye are better than the fowls. Trustful ease. God's care for the birds assures us of His care for us.
Take no thought for life; consider the ravens, which God feeds; ye are better than the fowls. Trustful ease. God's care for the birds assures us of His care for us.
Worry adds nothing; consider the lilies; if God so clothe the grass, much more you, 'O ye of little faith.' Provision promised. Anxiety betrays a small view of God.
Worry adds nothing; consider the lilies; if God so clothe the grass, much more you, 'O ye of little faith.' Provision promised. Anxiety betrays a small view of God.
Worry adds nothing; consider the lilies; if God so clothe the grass, much more you, 'O ye of little faith.' Provision promised. Anxiety betrays a small view of God.
Worry adds nothing; consider the lilies; if God so clothe the grass, much more you, 'O ye of little faith.' Provision promised. Anxiety betrays a small view of God.
Seek not anxiously what to eat; 'seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added.' First things first. Put God before all, and trust Him with the rest.
Seek not anxiously what to eat; 'seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added.' First things first. Put God before all, and trust Him with the rest.
Seek not anxiously what to eat; 'seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added.' First things first. Put God before all, and trust Him with the rest.
'Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom'; lay up treasure in heaven. Glad assurance. The Father delights to give His own the kingdom.
'Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom'; lay up treasure in heaven. Glad assurance. The Father delights to give His own the kingdom.
'Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom'; lay up treasure in heaven. Glad assurance. The Father delights to give His own the kingdom.
Let your loins be girded and lights burning; blessed are those servants the lord finds watching. Ready waiting. The faithful are always prepared for their Lord's return.
Let your loins be girded and lights burning; blessed are those servants the lord finds watching. Ready waiting. The faithful are always prepared for their Lord's return.
Let your loins be girded and lights burning; blessed are those servants the lord finds watching. Ready waiting. The faithful are always prepared for their Lord's return.
Let your loins be girded and lights burning; blessed are those servants the lord finds watching. Ready waiting. The faithful are always prepared for their Lord's return.
Had the goodman known the thief's hour, he would have watched; 'be ye therefore ready.' Constant readiness. The hour is unknown, so we must always be prepared.
Had the goodman known the thief's hour, he would have watched; 'be ye therefore ready.' Constant readiness. The hour is unknown, so we must always be prepared.
Blessed the faithful steward who gives the household their portion in due season. Faithful service. The diligent servant is honoured at his master's return.
Blessed the faithful steward who gives the household their portion in due season. Faithful service. The diligent servant is honoured at his master's return.
Blessed the faithful steward who gives the household their portion in due season. Faithful service. The diligent servant is honoured at his master's return.
Blessed the faithful steward who gives the household their portion in due season. Faithful service. The diligent servant is honoured at his master's return.
The evil servant who beats his fellows shall be cut off; to whom much is given, much is required. Greater accountability. Greater privilege brings greater responsibility.
The evil servant who beats his fellows shall be cut off; to whom much is given, much is required. Greater accountability. Greater privilege brings greater responsibility.
The evil servant who beats his fellows shall be cut off; to whom much is given, much is required. Greater accountability. Greater privilege brings greater responsibility.
The evil servant who beats his fellows shall be cut off; to whom much is given, much is required. Greater accountability. Greater privilege brings greater responsibility.
He came to send fire, and division, even within households, for His sake. Costly loyalty. Allegiance to Christ may divide the dearest ties.
He came to send fire, and division, even within households, for His sake. Costly loyalty. Allegiance to Christ may divide the dearest ties.
He came to send fire, and division, even within households, for His sake. Costly loyalty. Allegiance to Christ may divide the dearest ties.
He came to send fire, and division, even within households, for His sake. Costly loyalty. Allegiance to Christ may divide the dearest ties.
He came to send fire, and division, even within households, for His sake. Costly loyalty. Allegiance to Christ may divide the dearest ties.
Ye discern the sky but not 'this time'; ye hypocrites. Blind to the obvious. They read the weather but not the signs of God's working.
Ye discern the sky but not 'this time'; ye hypocrites. Blind to the obvious. They read the weather but not the signs of God's working.
Ye discern the sky but not 'this time'; ye hypocrites. Blind to the obvious. They read the weather but not the signs of God's working.
Agree with thine adversary on the way, lest he deliver thee to the judge. Settle quickly. Wisdom makes peace before judgment overtakes.
Agree with thine adversary on the way, lest he deliver thee to the judge. Settle quickly. Wisdom makes peace before judgment overtakes.
